perturbation.original_text: Each Party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any third party to whom such Party has provided Confidential Information, or any portion thereof. If a Party or any third party to whom such Party has provided Confidential Information becomes legally compelled (by oral question, deposition, interrogatory, request for documents, subpoena, civil investigative demand or similar process or by rule, regulation or other applicable law) to disclose any Confidential Information, such Party shall promptly notify the other Party of such requirement before any disclosure is made so that the other Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or may waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement. If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if a Party waives compliance with the provisions hereof, each Party agrees that only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is legally required to disclose (as advised by a written opinion of counsel) will be disclosed, and each Party agrees to exercise its best efforts to obtain assurance that the Confidential Information will be treated confidentially upon disclosur e.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party agrees to be responsible for any breach of this Agreement by any third party to whom such Party has provided Confidential Information, or any portion thereof. If a Party or any third party to whom such Party has provided Confidential Information becomes legally compelled to disclose any Confidential Information, such Party agrees that only that portion of the Confidential Information which it is legally required to disclose will be disclosed, and each Party agrees to exercise its best efforts to obtain assurance that the Confidential Information will be treated confidentially upon disclosure.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'such Party shall promptly notify the other Party of such requirement before any disclosure is made so that the other Party may seek a protective order or other appropriate remedy or may waive compliance with the terms of this Agreement. If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or if a Party waives compliance with the provisions hereof', the section now contradicts the intent of the agreement. While a party is still responsible for breaches, the other party is no longer given the opportunity to prevent a disclosure. This directly contradicts the idea of 'responsible handling' from section 1 where the standard for treatment of confidential information is reduced, creating uncertainty regarding the party’s action, when Confidential Information is being required by law.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Treatment of Confidential Information. The Confidential Information, whether transmitted orally, in writing or in any other form, and whether prepared by a Party or its employees, agents, advisors or other representatives, shall be handled responsibly by the receiving Party. Disclosure of the Confidential Information may be made only to employees, agents or independent contractors of a Party who are directly involved in consideration of the transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires a party to notify the other party before disclosing confidential information if legally compelled. The changed text removes this notification requirement. The contradicted text states that confidential information 'shall be handled responsibly by the receiving Party' and limits disclosure. By removing the notification requirement, the changed text allows for a scenario where a party discloses confidential information without giving the other party a chance to protect it, contradicting the requirement for 'responsible handling' and creating a direct conflict with the limited disclosure provision.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement will continue in full force and effect until both parties agree in writing to terminate this agreement and for one -year thereafter. The Parties agree that if the negotiated transaction does not proceed within a reasonable time, or upon any request from either Party, each Party shall promptly deliver to the other Party all written Confidential Information and any other written material containing or reflecting Confidential Information and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. All documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by each Party, or respective representatives, based on the information included in the Confidential Information shall be pro mptly destroyed, and such destruction shall be ce1tified in writing by an authorized officer supervising such destruction. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of any Confidential Information, each Party shall continue to be bound by its obligations o f confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement will continue in full force and effect until both parties agree in writing to terminate this agreement. The Parties agree that if the negotiated transaction does not proceed within a reasonable time, or upon any request from either Party, each Party shall promptly deliver to the other Party all written Confidential Information and any other written material containing or reflecting Confidential Information and will not retain any copies, extracts or other reproductions in whole or in part of such written material. All documents, memoranda, notes and other writings whatsoever prepared by each Party, or respective representatives, based on the information included in the Confidential Information shall be promptly destroyed.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'and for one -year thereafter' and 'and such destruction shall be ce1tified in writing by an authorized officer supervising such destruction. Notwithstanding the return or destruction of any Confidential Information, each Party shall continue to be bound by its obligations o f confidentiality and other obligations hereunder.', it becomes unclear when the confidentiality obligations cease, especially in light of the earlier change to 'handled responsibly' instead of 'preserved in strict confidence'. This contradicts the intent of the agreement. There is no way now to enforce destruction, or provide any evidence to show that the confidential information would remain confidential, creating uncertainty to the agreement’s terms.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Treatment of Confidential Information. The Confidential Information, whether transmitted orally, in writing or in any other form, and whether prepared by a Party or its employees, agents, advisors or other representatives, shall be handled responsibly by the receiving Party. Disclosure of the Confidential Information may be made only to employees, agents or independent contractors of a Party who are directly involved in consideration of the transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a one-year continuation of the agreement after termination and required certified destruction of confidential information. The changed text removes both clauses. This omission contradicts the 'Treatment of Confidential Information' clause, which implies ongoing responsible handling of confidential information. Without the one-year extension and destruction certification, it becomes unclear when the confidentiality obligations cease and how the 'handled responsibly' standard is to be enforced. This creates uncertainty and a strong contradiction, fitting the definition of 'Omissions – In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties hereby acknowledge and agree that, in the event of any breach of this Agreement by either Party, including, without limitation, the actual or threatened disclosure of a Party's Confidential Information without the express prior written consent of that Paity, such Party will suffer irreparable harm and injury and no remedy at law will afford it adequate protection against, or appropriate compensation for, such injury . Accordingly , each Party hereby agrees that, in any such event, the harmed Party shall be entitled to seek specific performance of the other Party's obligations under this Agreement , as well as such further injunctive relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties hereby acknowledge and agree that, in the event of any breach of this Agreement by either Party, including, without limitation, the actual or threatened disclosure of a Party's Confidential Information , each Party hereby agrees that, in any such event, the harmed Party shall be entitled to seek specific performance of the other Party's obligations under this Agreement , as well as such further injunctive relief as may be granted by a court of competent jurisdiction.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'without the express prior written consent of that Paity, such Party will suffer irreparable harm and injury and no remedy at law will afford it adequate protection against, or appropriate compensation for, such injury . Accordingly', removes the necessity of evidence of suffering injury. The harmed party is entitled to seek specific performance, this removal, creates a ambiguity to the agreement, in the case of an unwanted event, it creates a condition, where an injury must happen. This directly contradicts the idea of 'responsible handling' from section 1, creating uncertainty regarding the actions to be taken.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Treatment of Confidential Information. The Confidential Information, whether transmitted orally, in writing or in any other form, and whether prepared by a Party or its employees, agents, advisors or other representatives, shall be handled responsibly by the receiving Party. Disclosure of the Confidential Information may be made only to employees, agents or independent contractors of a Party who are directly involved in consideration of the transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that a breach, particularly the disclosure of confidential information, would cause irreparable harm and injury, requiring no remedy at law to provide adequate protection. By removing this clause in the changed text, it now allows the harmed party to seek specific performance without needing to demonstrate irreparable harm. This contradicts the 'responsible handling' clause in the contradicted text, as responsible handling should prevent irreparable harm in the first place, and the altered text now allows for specific performance even if the breach doesn't cause such harm. This creates a direct contradiction, impacting the enforceability and interpretation of obligations related to confidential information, and is therefore a strong, in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Unless otherwise required by law, without the prior written consent of the other Party, neither a Party nor its representatives shall disclose to any person (including any corporation, company, partnership or i ndividual) either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between the Parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such possible transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.changed_text: Unless otherwise required by law, neither a Party nor its representatives shall disclose to any person (including any corporation, company, partnership or i ndividual) either the fact that discussions or negotiations are taking place concerning a possible transaction between the Parties or any of the terms, conditions or other facts with respect to any such possible transaction, including the status thereof.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause 'without the prior written consent of the other Party', this section now contradicts with the idea that information must be handled responsibly. This contradicts 'handled responsibly' in Section 1 because it eliminates the need to obtain written consent, potentially leading to irresponsible dissemination of information. This directly introduces an ambiguity and in text contradiction to the agreement by removing the need of an approval, allowing the uncontrolled and unrestricted divulgation of negotiations or discussions.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Treatment of Confidential Information. The Confidential Information, whether transmitted orally, in writing or in any other form, and whether prepared by a Party or its employees, agents, advisors or other representatives, shall be handled responsibly by the receiving Party. Disclosure of the Confidential Information may be made only to employees, agents or independent contractors of a Party who are directly involved in consideration of the transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires 'prior written consent' before disclosing information, aligning with responsible handling of confidential information. The changed text removes this requirement, directly contradicting the 'handled responsibly' clause in the contradicted text. The removal of the consent requirement allows for unrestricted disclosure, which is incompatible with the obligation to handle confidential information responsibly, thus introducing a direct contradiction.
